calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-14-2026 Continued To Jul-01-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production To Defendant The Regents Of University Of California, Set One; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS JASPREET KALRA, and NAVDEEP SINGH'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production To Defendant The Regents Of University Of California, Set One; Request For Sanctions.

Plaintiffs' request to compel the production of the performance reviews, competency evaluations, disciplinary records, and training records in the personnel file of Dr. Ransohoff is granted. All these records must be produced to plaintiffs with a verification that all have been produced and none withheld no later than April 30, 2026. Plaintiffs may only use the records for this case and may not disseminate any of the records to anyone other than their experts without agreement of the Regents or court order and must destroy the records when the case has concluded. The records sought by this remaining portion of plaintiffs' motion to compel are all directly relevant and the Regents have not identified any particularized reason why any of the records deserve greater privacy protection than other confidential records. The Regents may not withhold any of the records on the grounds of EvidenceCode 1157 since their counsel stated at the last hearing that none of the records were protected by that statute.. Plaintiffs' request for monetary sanctions is denied since the Regents did not act without substantial justification. Counsel for the plaintiffs must prepare an order reflecting all of the rulings on this motion including the rulings made at the last hearing.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-08-2026 Continued To Jun-09-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: David Marcolini, Esq., specially appearing for the plaintiff. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to June 09, 2026, at 10:30am in Department 610. Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk Z. Dizon; Proceedings not reported. (610)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production To Defendant The Regents Of University Of California, Set One; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-24-2026 Continued To Apr-15-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S JASPREET KALRA'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION TO DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, SET ONE; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS

Appearances via Zoom:
Alena Klimianok of Klimianok & Merzel (310.312.0220, km@kmlitigation.com) for plaintiff and moving party;
Brian M. Davies of Hassard Bonnington LLP (415.288.9800, bmd@hassard.com) for defendant and responding party.

Court and counsel discuss the remaining issues provided in the parties' email update received yesterday. Defendant is to provide a privelege log by April 3, 2026, that complies with the parameters discussed. The court continues the matter to April 15, 2026, for further hearing. Counsel to file further briefing as to whether records in personnel file that are pre-incident having to do with performance review, competency evaluation, disciplinary records, application and training should be discoverable. Defendant to file their brief by April 3, 2026, and plaintiff to respond by April 9, 2026.

Counsel confirm they have resolved issues regarding supplemental responses.The court will defer the issue of sanctions to April 15, 2026.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production To Defendant The Regents Of University Of California, Set One; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-16-2026 Continued To Mar-18-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S JASPREET KALRA'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION TO DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, SET ONE; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: continued to March 18, 2026 to give the parties one more opportunity to resolve the issues raised by this motion. The parties are required to meet and confer in person or by remote video platform no later than 5pm on March 16, 2026 in an effort to resolve all remaining issues raised by this motion. No later than 4pm on March 17 counsel for plaintiffs must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties succinctly stating, without argument, each remaining unresolved issue raised by thism motion. For guidance of the parties, the court believes that a party responding to document requests must identify, as to each request, whether and why any responsive documents have not and will not be produced and, if the reason for the non-production is an assertion of privilege, the following information about each withheld document other than documents to, from or written by outside litigation counsel after the case was filed must be provided: date of document, ground(s) of privilege, type of document (e.g. letter, memo), author(s) of document, all recipients of documents, and a brief description of the document. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
